seoTitle: Mount David vs Mount Druitt — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Mount David and Mount Druitt. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Mount David vs Mount Druitt: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mount David and Mount Druitt.
heading: Mount David vs Mount Druitt — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mount David, New South Wales and Mount Druitt, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mount David has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Mount Druitt (46/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mount David or Mount Druitt safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mount David has a flood risk score of 38/100, while Mount Druitt scores 35/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mount David scores 57/100 for bushfire risk. Mount Druitt scores 45/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mount David or Mount Druitt?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mount David has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.42% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
